title: Junk Food Is Now a Staple of Many Americans' Diets
subtitle: 垃圾食品如何成为美国人的主食

paragraphs.en: Junk food is now a staple of many Americans' diets. Advertising campaigns from the snack food companies, often featuring sports stars, send the message that we can neutralize any negative effects of consuming their products simply by getting more physical exercise. But recent studies show a lack of exercise is not to blame for rising obesity rates. Bad diets are. Interventions to help reduce junk food consumption are especially important for children and teenagers.
paragraphs.zh: 垃圾食品如今已成为许多美国人饮食的主食。休闲食品公司的广告活动通常以体育明星为主角，传递出这样一个信息：只需增加体育锻炼，便能消除食用其产品带来的任何负面影响。然而，近期研究表明，运动不足并非肥胖率上升的元凶，不良饮食才是。帮助减少垃圾食品摄入的干预措施，对儿童和青少年而言尤为重要。

paragraphs.en: Prevention is better than cure because obesity is so difficult to treat. Unfortunately, while health education has shown some success among young children, teens have been harder to reach. Now a large-scale study has tried an innovative approach to change teenagers' attitudes towards healthy eating, and the results are promising.
paragraphs.zh: 预防胜于治疗，因为肥胖一旦形成极难逆转。遗憾的是，尽管健康教育在幼儿群体中取得了一定成效，青少年群体却难以触达。如今，一项大规模研究尝试了一种创新方法来改变青少年对健康饮食的态度，结果令人鼓舞。

paragraphs.en: The researchers argued that previous interventions have probably been unsuccessful because of a major flaw: they focused on a future, healthier you and assumed that this would be enough motivation for teenagers. In contrast, the new intervention cleverly exploits teenagers' instinct for rebelliousness and autonomy, and the value they place on social justice.
paragraphs.zh: 研究人员认为，以往的干预措施之所以收效甚微，很可能源于一个重大缺陷：它们着眼于描绘一个未来更健康的自我，并假定这足以激励青少年。相比之下，新的干预措施巧妙地利用了青少年的叛逆本能与自主意识，以及他们对社会公正的重视。

paragraphs.en: To do this, researchers had students read an article on the food industry. It revealed a secret about the manipulative and deceptive strategies used to make junk food more addictive and characterize the products as healthy. The article also explained how advertising campaigns specifically target very young and poor people, causing harm for these vulnerable groups.
paragraphs.zh: 为此，研究人员让学生阅读一篇关于食品行业的文章。文章揭露了一个秘密：食品公司采用操纵性和欺骗性策略，使垃圾食品更具成瘾性，并将其包装成健康产品。文章还阐明了广告活动如何专门针对幼儿和贫困群体，给这些弱势群体造成伤害。

words.meaning: adj. 操控性的；有操纵意图的
adj. 脆弱的；易受伤害的

paragraphs.en: Afterwards, the participants read a fictional survey of teens who wanted to "fight back against the companies by buying and eating less processed food". After the intervention, participants associated healthy eating with autonomy and social justice. The teenagers also rated healthy eating as being more appealing.
paragraphs.zh: 随后，参与者阅读了一份虚构的青少年调查，调查中的受访者表示希望"通过少购买、少食用加工食品来反击这些公司"。干预结束后，参与者开始将健康饮食与自主权和社会公正联系起来，并认为健康饮食更具吸引力。

paragraphs.en: Importantly, there were also some promising effects of the new intervention on actual behavior. A day later the students were offered a choice of snacks and drinks in a seemingly unrelated context. The teens chose healthy snacks and drinks (such as fruit or water) more often over unhealthy options (like biscuits and soda). Apparently associating a healthy diet with teenagers' own values seems to be a promising avenue to prevent obesity.
paragraphs.zh: 更重要的是，新的干预措施对实际行为也产生了可喜的影响。一天后，学生们在一个看似无关的情境中被提供了零食和饮料的选择。青少年更频繁地选择健康零食和饮料（如水果或白水），而非不健康的选项（如饼干和苏打水）。将健康饮食与青少年自身价值观相结合，显然是一条预防肥胖的有望路径。
